title: Will the Fed Tighten Soon So It Can Ease Even More Later?

Darius Dale of 42 Macro, says the Fed may elect to tighten now so it can ease [music] even more later. Investors should buy the dip but be patient. Trees don't grow to the moon even if there will eventually be data centers in space. Darius joins us now from more. Darius, good to see you, buddy. >> Great to see you guys. >> Should I be worried about the outlook for this Federal Reserve? >> Yeah, not worried. I think they're, you know, I I said this with Ty earlier. I think they're going to play action pass so they can set up the run. They have to spook the markets and and regain some of that credibility on inflation fighting just so that they can create more scope to ease monetary policy in the ensuing years. >> Let me just jump in. Is it a communication exercise? Can you achieve that through communication alone or do you need to actually hike interest rates? >> Uh I I think interest rates are the wrong tool. I think it's a a balance sheet exercise in our opinion because we think they need to target the speculative excesses at the top part of the K-shaped US economy. And so right now the Fed's got to take reserve management purchases to zero and threaten the market that they may reinstate balance sheet runoff in order to take some of the, you know, the pressure out of financial markets that's, you know, leading companies like Apple to tighten >> Is that what you're expected to see this summer or is that too soon? >> In our opinion, based on our outlook for what the likely, you know, sort of net result of the five task forces is likely to be, we think it'll be a dovish net result. Not all dovish but but a dovish net result. So in order for them to get create scope for that, you know, a kind of create a runway for that, they're going to have to take some steam off the boil from an inflation fighting perspective in the coming months. >> How exactly would that be effectuated with the balance sheet and where in the risk asset market would you actually see the ramifications of that? >> if the S&P 500 is down 10 to 15% and the Fed says, "Okay, enough is enough." You take the steam off the boil. I mean, right now we're annualizing on PCE core PC inflation at 3.5%, super core north of 4%. Now these are some pretty uncomfortable numbers and so ultimately we know we're at peak inflation those numbers are going to come down but the rate of change of those that that that's deceleration could be an issue for the market in the context of what we just saw with Apple. Fun fact, I wrote my senior thesis at Yale about how a market-based economy can't have a single payer health care system because it would drain resources from the other sectors of the economy and push up inflation. That's exactly what's happening right now with AI and and and and Apple. >> So, right now that's part of the reason why you're seeing inflation where it is and you're saying that in order to lower that inflation, you need to see some sort of significant drawdown in equity markets. How are you positioning ahead of that or how are you recommending positioning ahead of that? >> Well, the the first step of the trade and we think it's already on is the source of funds trade where the investors are using, you know, these sort of AI providers, particularly the hyperscalers, to capitalize the AI adopters and and the businesses in the economy that have been left behind, not just in this particular market cycle, but for, you know, 10-15 years. In our opinion, we think that trade is durable. We talked about it last time we were on the show. Uh we think that trade is durable. It has multi-year legs, but if the market's down, you know, 10-15% in S&P terms this summer or this fall, nothing's going to work. I mean, >> [laughter] >> you know, what do you hide out? Yeah, volatility. >> Darius is going to see you in a minute. Darius, it's buddy. Thank you, buddy. Darius Dale There, 42 Macro. Just another dimension, another way of thinking about this Federal Reserve. And there's a range of views right now because a lot of people are filling in the blanks because there were so many in the news conference just last week. >> Yeah, we're all moonlighting as task force task force members because ultimately we want to know the answers. That said, is this a feature or a bug? The lack of understanding of the of the of the response mechanism for the Federal Reserve. And ultimately, you got people lining up on both sides. >> But Jonna, thank you asked the right question. Can you set deterrence alone just with communication or do they actually need to deliver on that hike to actually show they really do care about getting inflation closer to target? >> Yeah, there's a sense they might have to deliver. For now though, the equity markets have joined what is got. We're up by 8/10 of 1% on the S&P 500. Yields are declining. Inflation is small, very small downside surprise. The debate continues
